title: `unique` annotation on `if` generates WARNING-LATCH
body:
**Version**: Verilator 4.200 2021-03-12 rev v4.110-43-g5022e81af (mod)

**Test case**:
```
module main (
    input logic go
);
    logic [1:0] cs_if_in;
    logic [1:0] fsm_out;
    always_comb begin
        unique if(fsm_out == 2'd1) cs_if_in = 2'd1;
        else if (fsm_out == 2'd2) cs_if_in = 2'd2;
        else cs_if_in = 2'd0;
    end
endmodule

```

**To reproduce**: run `verilator --lint-only out.sv`.
**Expected**: No warnings are reported
**Output**: 
```
%Warning-LATCH: out.sv:7:5: Latch inferred for signal 'main.cs_if_in' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)
                          : ... Suggest use of always_latch for intentional latches
    7 |     always_comb begin
      |     ^~~~~~~~~~~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off LATCH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```

Removing the `unique` in front of `if` eliminates the error. I'm not sure if I'm misunderstanding how `unique if` works but I had expected that the final `else` branch in the `if` would mean that a latch would not have be inferred here.

Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?
Yes
